fed_press_releases.title: Federal Reserve keeps policy settings unchanged
fed_press_releases.summary: Officials said inflation progress remains uneven while liquidity conditions stay orderly.
fed_press_releases.body_text: Federal Reserve officials kept policy settings unchanged and said inflation progress remains uneven while balance sheet runoff and liquidity conditions stay orderly.

us_bls_news.title: Employment growth slows in February
us_bls_news.summary: Payroll growth moderated and wage gains cooled.
us_bls_news.body_text: Payroll growth moderated in February and wage growth cooled, suggesting labor demand is easing without a sharp deterioration in employment.

us_bea_news.title: Consumer spending growth softens
us_bea_news.summary: Personal income rose while real spending growth softened.
us_bea_news.body_text: Personal income rose in February while real consumer spending growth softened, pointing to steadier but slower household demand.

reuters_business.title: Investors weigh softer growth against steady policy
reuters_business.summary: Bond investors questioned whether softer growth justified steady policy expectations.
reuters_business.body_text: Bond investors questioned whether softer growth justified steady Federal Reserve expectations as Treasury yields drifted lower and equities traded unevenly.

wsj_markets.title: Markets look for confirmation that growth is cooling
wsj_markets.summary: Investors watched for confirmation that growth is cooling without tipping into contraction.

jpmorgan_am_research.headline: J.P. Morgan AM says a minority of allocators still expect a harder landing
jpmorgan_am_research.snippet: A minority of allocators still expects a harder landing and said investors should watch next inflation data.
jpmorgan_am_research.text: J.P. Morgan Asset Management said a minority of allocators still expects a harder landing, challenged the quick-cut consensus, and told investors to watch next inflation data and labor-market revisions.
